page: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks falls into the top 10%, while large block read and write bandwidths remain strong in the top 25%. Geekbench testing places software compilation and image processing in the top 10%, with ray tracing and text processing in the top 25%. For database operations, Redis SET throughput is in the top 10%, and Passmark database operations are in the top 25%. Large-model LLM inference drops to average performance, but smaller models show top-tier to strong execution speeds.

The F48als_v6 offers high compute density and strong raw processing power per resource unit, making it a cost-efficient option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the low memory allocation of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the complete lack of local ephemeral storage, which requires external premium storage attachment. This profile is suitable for software compilation, batch processing, image processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It contains no local storage or GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. The primary tradeoff is the low memory density and lack of local storage. This instance is suited for CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, image processing, web serving, and small-to-medium model LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for CPU-intensive workloads and compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core processing performance.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a low memory-to-CPU ratio for high-throughput processing.

96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and Redis performance
No local storage or GPU resources included
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and web serving
